topic: Claude Code vs Codex CLI vs Gemini CLI 2026: Terminal AI Agents Compared
keyword: claude code vs codex cli vs gemini cli

competitors.title: Claude Code vs Codex CLI vs Gemini CLI (2026 Comparison) - DeployHQ
key_points: Claude Code: TypeScript, proprietary, 200K context, no free tier, $20/mo Pro; best for multi-file editing and codebase understanding
Codex CLI: Rust + TypeScript, Apache 2.0, 200K context, no free tier (needs ChatGPT sub), $20/mo Plus; best for single-file tasks and CI/CD integration with native GitHub Actions support
Gemini CLI: TypeScript, Apache 2.0, 1M context, free tier (1,000 req/day), free/$20/mo AI Pro; best for Google Search grounding and large context codebases
All three support MCP, custom system instructions (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md), and sandboxed execution
Real-world tests: Claude Code most thorough (asks clarifying questions, ~90s), Codex CLI fastest (~45s), Gemini CLI most current via Google Search (~60s)
Codex CLI excels at CI/CD with native GitHub Actions and async cloud execution; Claude Code best for coherent multi-file refactoring; Gemini CLI best for staying current

competitors.title: Claude Code vs Codex vs Gemini CLI: Feature Comparison - IntuitionLabs
key_points: Claude Code built on Claude Opus 4.x-5.x with agentic search, 1M token context (via Opus 4.6), and Compaction API for infinite conversations
Codex CLI defaults to GPT-5.3 Codex (400K input/128K output), scored 77.3% on Terminal-Bench 2.0 vs Claude's 65.4%
Claude Code leads SWE-Bench bug fixing at 80.8% and handles complex multi-step workflows with fewer syntax errors
Gemini CLI has ~40-50% higher error rate than Claude but praised for raw throughput and speed
Claude Code: 115,000 developers, 195M lines of code processed weekly within 4 months of launch; Codex CLI: ~61K GitHub stars; Gemini CLI: ~60K stars
Claude Code dominates share of voice: 75% of coding-agent discussions on social media vs Codex 22% and Gemini CLI 3%
Enterprise case studies: Stripe 10K-line Scala→Java migration in 4 days via Claude Code; Ramp 80% faster incident response; Wiz 50K-line Python→Go in ~20 active hours
73% of engineering teams use AI coding tools daily, with Claude leading for complex tasks (44% of teams)

competitors.title: AI Coding Assistant Statistics 2026: 50+ Key Data Points - Uvik
key_points: 84% developer adoption of AI coding tools (up from lower baseline); 29% trust AI output (down from 40%)
$2B+ Cursor ARR; 46% of GitHub Copilot-generated code committed; code churn increased 3.1% to 5.7%
METR trial showed experienced developers 19% slower using AI despite feeling 20% faster
AI code assistant market at $6B in 2026 growing at 22% CAGR; 1.3M GitHub Copilot subscribers

angles: The trust gap: 84% adoption but only 29% trust — why developers use terminal AI agents but hesitate to trust them fully
Three approval philosophies: Claude Code's permission prompts vs Codex CLI's graduated Suggest/Auto-Edit/Full Auto vs Gemini CLI's configurable trust + Yolo mode
Open source vs proprietary: Codex CLI and Gemini CLI (Apache 2.0) vs Claude Code (closed) — what enterprises need to know
Context window wars: 1M tokens (Gemini CLI, Claude Opus 4.6) vs 400K (Codex) — does bigger context mean better code?
CI/CD-native coding: Codex CLI's GitHub Actions integration and async cloud execution for automated deployment workflows
The multi-tool reality: Why many developers use 2-3 terminal AI agents for different tasks instead of committing to one
Compaction and infinite conversations: How Claude Code's semantic summarization enables longer, more productive coding sessions
